description: Official Lados workflow control primitives for triggers, branching, parallel flow, delay, and logging.
guardrails: Workflow controls must not approve, certify, or decide commercial entitlement.
Event publishing must preserve source resource and actor metadata when available.

verification.manifest: verified — S9.1 fix: the skeleton declared "workflow.control.loop" and "workflow.event.publish" capabilities with no corresponding nodes; added lados.workflow.loop and lados.workflow.publish_event to close both gaps. "workflow.trigger.event" remains capability-only by design — event-triggered workflow starts are handled by EventBusService subscription dispatch (see event-bus.service.ts), not by a runnable node; a placeholder node would have no executable behavior of its own. Phase 22 S22.4 (2026-07-06): added "workflow.control.switch" + lados.workflow.switch (true multi-way routing, 5 fixed case slots + default — see switch.ts header for why fixed rather than dynamic) and extended lados.workflow.condition's expression grammar (named fields + AND/OR combinator, backward compatible). Phase 23 S23.3 (2026-07-08): added "workflow.pipeline.save_artifact"/"workflow.pipeline.read_artifact" + lados.workflow.pipeline_save_artifact/pipeline_read_artifact — cross-workflow data handoff scoped to one pipeline_run_id (migration 0075's pipeline_artifacts table), successor in spirit to the dead project.save_artifact/project.read_artifact (archived with lados.core-pack in Phase 21 S9) but architecturally new, not a resurrection. Phase 24 S24.3 (2026-07-11): renamed to "workflow.program.save_artifact"/"workflow.program.read_artifact" + lados.workflow.program_save_artifact/program_read_artifact — corporate-vocabulary rename (Pipeline→Program), same table/column targets as migration 0079 renamed them to (program_run_id, program_artifacts). Old node type strings preserved as compatibility aliases, see @lados/pack-sdk's compatibility-aliases.ts.
verification.runtime: implemented — 12 of 13 capabilities have a backing node (see manifest note above for the one intentional exception, workflow.trigger.event)
